scratch: Sprint 9 queue seeded 2026-07-21 after Sprint 8 merged (PR #42, v3.4.1). NOT YET LAUNCHED - the human dev launching this sprint runs /loop-sprint-cron THEMSELVES; agents never start the loop. THEME: ACTIVATE THE SEAM - hybrid Keycloak OIDC exchange (flag-gated default OFF), discard-draft affordance across all four services, demo/export automation. Version at close 3.5.0 (tag/CHANGELOG only - do NOT touch package manifests). Decisions land in-task: D-031 discard (T0), D-032 hybrid OIDC (T4), D-033 opt-in destructive automation (T8). One migration: 0032_user_keycloak_sub (T4, additive, batch_alter_table). SHIELD_AUTH_OIDC_ENABLED stays FALSE for the default suite - flipped only inside T6 hand-verify / T7 opt-in e2e, then RESTORED and one credentials sign-in re-proven. REQUIRE_MFA / REQUIRE_EMAIL_VERIFY stay FALSE as always. T2 adds pypdf to api dev deps -> docker compose build api (plain restart won't install it - the Sprint 7 google-auth lesson). T8's demo-reset run is DESTRUCTIVE (down -v): scheduled late, reseed + verify /ready + one sign-in afterward, never run it in any other task. Realm-export edits (T5) need a keycloak-data volume wipe to re-import. Every new spec self-skips by default: s26 needs E2E_OIDC=1, demo-journey needs SHIELD_DEMO_SMOKE=1 - prove the skip (suite count unchanged) before writing assertions. SIX gates unchanged. ruff==0.15.20/black==26.5.1 pinned + known-first-party=['app'] in ROOT pyproject - do not remove. gh auth switch before gh writes.
gates: export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T api pytest -m unit -q
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T web sh -lc "cd /app && pnpm -F web exec tsc --noEmit"

cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && NODE_DIR=$(ls -d "$LOCALAPPDATA/Microsoft/WinGet/Packages/OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS"*/node-v*-win-x64 2>/dev/null | head -1) && export PATH="$PATH:$NODE_DIR" && npx -y prettier@3.9.5 --check "**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,md,yml,yaml}"
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T api sh -lc "cd /app && ruff check --no-cache . && black --check ."
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T web sh -lc "cd /app && pnpm -F web test"
export PATH="$PATH:/c/Program Files/Docker/Docker/resources/bin" && cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && docker compose exec -T web sh -lc "cd /app && pnpm -F web lint"
notes_global: e2e on the HOST via the winget node.exe + e2e/node_modules/@playwright/test/cli.js; base URL from root .env WEB_PORT via e2e/helpers/baseUrl.ts (Dave's box :3001, canonical/CI :3000). Poll long gates (pytest, e2e) in the FOREGROUND to the end of the iteration in sub-9-minute bursts - NEVER park on background monitors. Docker CLI needs the PATH export per shell. After ANY apps/web or packages/* edit: docker compose up -d --force-recreate web BEFORE e2e (T1 and T6 touch web). New python module under app/ (T4: app/security/oidc.py + app/routes/oidc.py) needs docker compose restart api; NEVER restart api mid-pytest (SIGKILL 137). pyproject change (T2 pypdf) needs docker compose build api. Migration 0032 (T4): docker compose exec -T api alembic upgrade head before any e2e that signs in. Flipping SHIELD_AUTH_OIDC_ENABLED requires docker compose up -d --force-recreate api web (web reads it at provider registration, api at boot readiness) - always restore flag-off and re-prove one credentials sign-in before ending the task. Keycloak realm re-import: docker compose stop keycloak && docker volume rm <project>_keycloak-data && docker compose up -d keycloak. Seed idempotent: docker compose exec -T api python scripts/seed_demo.py. Logins admin@kentro.example / client@atlas.example, DemoPass!2026; spec-created users need unique timestamped emails. Auth.js v5: MFA sign-in signal is signIn(...).code === 'mfa_required', NOT .error; specs drive the UI, never call signIn() directly. LLM fixture-mode default (D-017) - no live env needed this sprint. Tenant enforcement: require_*_in_tenant 404s (never 403); resolve seeded ids ONLY via e2e/helpers/ids.ts. Playwright gotchas: getByRole name matching is SUBSTRING; click()+waitForResponse not check() on auto-save; assert post-Run-AI state after page.reload(); no body click before first Tab in skip-link tests; full-suite e2e is overload-prone on this box - per-spec STANDALONE re-run is the flake arbiter.

tasks.title: Backend discard endpoints x4 + DISCARDED status + version-numbering fix (D-031)
verify: TDD-first in new tests/unit/test_discard_draft.py (copy the app_client fixture shape from test_attack_draft_guard.py) - watch each contract fail before implementing

POST .../{id}/discard per service (tech-debt/capability-lists, csf|attack|zt/assessments), mirroring each approve sibling's layout/RBAC (_admin_required): DRAFT -> 200 status='discarded' + ONE audit row (capability_list.discarded / csf|attack|zt.assessment.discarded, details incl. version + item/answer counts + answered_count for csf/zt); re-discard -> idempotent 200, NO second audit row; SUBMITTED (csf/zt) -> 409 typed {reason:'not_discardable'}; APPROVED/RELEASED -> 409; client role -> 403; unknown/cross-tenant id -> 404
DISCARDED added to all four status enums - these are native_enum=False String(16) columns with NO CHECK constraint (verified vs migration 0009), so NO migration; 'discarded' added nowhere else

VERSION TRAP regression: _latest_* helpers (tech_debt.py:121, csf.py:185, attack.py:141, zt.py:175) gain status != DISCARDED (covers reuse guard + GET latest + all consumers); the mint next-version computation (tech_debt.py:222, csf.py:391, attack.py:274, zt.py:555) switches to select(func.max(version)) WITHOUT the filter. Test: v1 approved -> v2 draft -> discard v2 -> mint = v3, no IntegrityError, on the alembic-upgraded SQLite fixture so (service_id, version) unique is real
after discard: GET latest 404s (or returns the prior approved version where one exists) and the create/extract POST mints a FRESH version 201 instead of the 200-reuse
tech-debt: intake artifacts SURVIVE discard; re-extract with the same artifact_id succeeds and fires the LLM seam exactly once

HIDDEN LATEST CONSUMERS (Codex blocker/should-fix): risk.py's own _latest() (risk.py:65-78) feeding _gate()/_gather_findings() (:125-162) must skip DISCARDED - regressions: finalized v1 + discarded v2 -> synthesis reads v1; discarded-only service does NOT unlock the gate. intake.py engagement cards (:299-306, :326) report 'latest non-discarded' - discarded-only and finalized-v1/discarded-v2 cases tested

CHILD MUTATIONS reject a discarded parent (Codex should-fix): audit every by-row-ID mutation + AI-run guard across the four services (e.g. zt.py:639-652, attack.py:480-487) - rule becomes 'parent in its editable state(s)', not 'not approved/released'; stale-tab PATCH-after-discard tests x4

CONCURRENCY CONTRACT (Codex blocker): discard write is a conditional UPDATE ... WHERE status='draft' (rows-affected drives 200/idempotent/409) so two transactions can't both observe DRAFT; child writers' parent-state guards make a racing client PATCH or AI run lose LOUDLY (typed 409); an AI run that loaded a DRAFT parent re-checks status before committing; discard-then-stale-write contract tests per service

client-touched csf/zt DRAFTS are discardable (Dave's call - the UI warns, T1); success-path logs techdebt_discarded_draft / csf_assessment_discarded / attack_assessment_discarded / zt_assessment_discarded
D-031 appended to DECISIONS.md incl. the conditional-update concurrency contract; pytest -m unit + ruff/black green
tasks.notes: See SPRINT_9.md T0. The version trap is the task's landmine - getting it wrong is an IntegrityError on the first re-extract after discarding a non-v1 draft.

tasks.title: Backend OIDC: flag + oidc_readiness, JWKS verifier, POST /auth/oidc/exchange, migration 0032 (D-032)
verify: TDD-first test_oidc_exchange.py: in-test RSA keypair signs Keycloak-shaped tokens, monkeypatched app.security.oidc._fetch_jwks returns the matching JWKS - watch the full rejection matrix fail before the route exists

SHIELD_AUTH_OIDC_ENABLED default False; oidc_readiness() mirrors live_llm_readiness() (config-shape only, NO network at boot - api has no depends_on keycloak and must not crash-loop on cold compose up): flag on requires non-empty http(s) keycloak_issuer + new keycloak_jwks_url setting + keycloak_audience + keycloak_client_id, else loud boot RuntimeError (test_config.py cases)

app/security/oidc.py: module-level JWKS cache {kid: jwk} + fetched-at, 300s TTL, threading.Lock, httpx GET 5s timeout, EXACTLY ONE forced refetch on unknown kid; raw fetch isolated in _fetch_jwks() for monkeypatching; NO new dependency (python-jose[cryptography] + httpx already ship)

POST /auth/oidc/exchange in new app/routes/oidc.py (registered in main.py) takes {keycloak_access_token} (the ACCESS token - it carries aud=shield-api via the realm mapper), returns {user, tokens}; typed dict-detail failures: 403 oidc_disabled / 401 oidc_token_invalid (RS256-only algorithms list = alg-confusion guard; iss=keycloak_issuer, aud=keycloak_audience, require exp/iat/sub; ALSO azp claim must equal keycloak_client_id - Codex finding: aud names the resource server, not the requesting client; test a correctly signed token minted to a DIFFERENT client is rejected) / 503 oidc_jwks_unavailable (names URL + flag) / 401 oidc_claims_missing / 403 oidc_email_unverified / 403 oidc_no_local_account (NO JIT provisioning; match by normalized verified email) / 403 oidc_user_inactive / 403 oidc_sub_mismatch

TOFU: users.keycloak_sub NULL -> stamped on first exchange (log oidc.sub_bound); set-and-different -> 403. Migration 0032: String(64) nullable UNIQUE via batch_alter_table, additive; alembic upgrade head clean in the api container

success reuses _issue_pair + _register_successful_login (promote to a shared helper only if ruff fights the private import): pair passes verify_token (HS256 iss=shield-api), /auth/refresh rotates it, lockout counters cleared, last_login_at stamped; a token claiming roles:['admin'] for a local client-role user mints CLIENT tokens (DB role authoritative); local lockout NOT consulted; local email_verified_at NOT stamped
flag default OFF: the entire pre-existing unit suite passes untouched; D-032 appended to DECISIONS.md; docker compose restart api after adding the new modules; pytest -m unit + ruff/black green
tasks.notes: See SPRINT_9.md T4. Keycloak tokens are NEVER accepted as API bearers - the exchange is the only door.

tasks.title: Infra: KC_HOSTNAME dual-horizon, realm drift fixes, env plumbing, real /ready probe
verify: TDD-first test_readiness.py: flag off -> keycloak 'dormant'/required=false (existing tests re-anchored, not weakened; detail names the flag); flag on + probe ok -> 'ok'; flag on + fetch raising -> 'down' AND ready stays true (keycloak NEVER gates readiness - credentials login keeps the app serviceable during a Keycloak outage)

compose keycloak service: KC_HOSTNAME=http://localhost:8080 + KC_HOSTNAME_BACKCHANNEL_DYNAMIC='true' (one canonical iss for browser AND containers; backchannel token/JWKS endpoints stay container-reachable); api-env gains SHIELD_AUTH_OIDC_ENABLED:-false + KEYCLOAK_JWKS_URL; web-env gains SHIELD_AUTH_OIDC_ENABLED:-false + KEYCLOAK_INTERNAL_ISSUER=http://keycloak:8080/realms/shield; KEYCLOAK_ISSUER default flipped to http://localhost:8080/realms/shield in compose + .env.example + config.py; docker-compose.demo.yml's web-env KEYCLOAK_ISSUER aligned too (Codex finding - the demo overlay would silently undo the flip); hand-check: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.demo.yml config shows the pinned issuer on api AND web

tasks.title: Web OIDC: conditional Keycloak provider, jwt-callback exchange branch, sign-in button, failure guard
verify: TDD-first vitest: rewriteKeycloakUrl rewrite/passthrough table; isOidcEnabled truth table; KeycloakSignInButton renders + calls mocked signIn('keycloak'); SessionExpiryGuard signs out to /sign-in?reason=oidc_exchange_failed on OIDC_EXCHANGE_ERROR

START WITH A MINIMAL AUTH-CODE SPIKE (Codex finding): prove redirect -> Keycloak form -> callback -> token round trip with the secret-less client BEFORE wiring the full callback branch, so the beta-sensitivity verdict lands in the first hour

providers: [Credentials, ...(isOidcEnabled() ? [keycloak] : [])] - flag off means the provider does NOT exist; keycloak config: public client + PKCE (checks ['pkce','state'], client.token_endpoint_auth_method 'none', customFetch = keycloakFetch rewriting only the public->internal issuer prefix for container-side discovery). PRE-APPROVED FALLBACK if next-auth 5.0.0-beta.31 fights the secret-less client, defined COMPLETELY (Codex finding): realm export publicClient:false + clientAuthenticatorType:'client-secret' + dev secret (dev-realm-only; documented gitleaks allowlist entry if flagged); KEYCLOAK_CLIENT_SECRET plumbed through BASE and DEMO compose; confidential-token-exchange e2e proof - document the flip in the commit body. Do NOT bump the next-auth beta in this task

jwt callback: keycloak branch ordered BEFORE the credentials if(user) seed (OIDC initial sign-in also carries a profile user); on success seed token.{role,accessToken,refreshToken,accessExpiresAt} from POST /auth/oidc/exchange with account.access_token - shape IDENTICAL to the credentials path so downstream expiry/refresh/session logic is untouched; on ApiError log the typed reason server-side and set token.error = OIDC_EXCHANGE_ERROR (new constant in lib/auth/errors.ts)

sign-in page: server component reads isOidcEnabled(), conditionally renders KeycloakSignInButton separated from the credentials form + a loud banner for reason=oidc_exchange_failed beside the existing session_expired one; flag off -> button absent, page byte-identical

THE HARDEST PROMISE - flag OFF is a BEHAVIORAL no-op (Codex rewording): provider absent, ZERO Keycloak discovery/JWKS network requests (vitest trap for unexpected fetches), every credential surface unchanged; full default e2e suite green + existing SignInForm/MFA vitest suites pass unchanged (options.ts is load-bearing for all ~24 credential specs)

PRODUCTION BUILD SURVIVES (Codex finding): in-container pnpm -F web build green - provider registration/env handling can typecheck clean and still die at Next build/start; mandatory for THIS task, not a new global gate

hand-verify with the flag flipped on BOTH containers (docker compose up -d --force-recreate api web), then RESTORED: admin@kentro.example full browser round trip lands authenticated with working API pages; nolocal@atlas.example lands on the loud banner; credentials + TOTP MFA sign-in re-verified working WITH the flag on (hybrid coexistence); after restore, one credentials sign-in re-proven
six gates green (tsc / vitest / eslint / prettier especially); docker compose up -d --force-recreate web before any e2e
tasks.notes: See SPRINT_9.md T6. THE sprint's highest-risk task - beta-version-sensitive at three points (secret-less client, customFetch, callback error semantics). One variable at a time; the fallback is pre-approved, not a halt condition.
